summary: Parallel review: FAIL (1 critical, 19 warning, 11 suggestion). Compound gate: PASS

findingsList.text: 🟡 `run.mjs:1608` — `_runSingleFeature` returns `"done"` when `simplifyBlocked=true` but `blocked===0`. The outer loop's >50% tasks-passed heuristic marks the roadmap item as done, bypassing the simplify block entirely.
🟡 `run.mjs:1507` — Feature state stays `"executing"` indefinitely when finalize is blocked — no terminal state transition occurs. Dashboard and crash recovery see a stale status.

🟡 `test/simplify-pass.test.mjs:360` — `--no-simplify` test replicates the conditional in test code rather than exercising the actual run.mjs code path. No integration test verifies `harness("finalize")` is NOT called when `simplifyBlocked=true`.
🟡 `simplify-pass.mjs:194` — `writeFileSync` for the eval artifact silently swallows errors. A FAIL verdict with no evidence file is an audit gap.

🟡 bin/lib/run.mjs:1562 — When `simplifyBlocked=true`, console still prints "Feature complete" and function returns `"done"` instead of `"blocked"`. Outer loop reads STATE.json so behavior is correct today, but console output misleads operators and return value is a latent contract issue.

🟡 bin/lib/run.mjs:1613 — `_runSingleFeature` returns `"done"` when `simplifyBlocked === true` and task-level `blocked === 0`. Outer loop misinterprets this as successful completion, but finalize was never called. Should return `"simplify-blocked"` or incorporate simplify state into the blocked check.

🟡 bin/lib/run.mjs:1562 — "Feature complete" header and notification fire unconditionally, even when simplification blocked finalize. User sees contradictory messages: "blocking finalize" then "Feature complete."
- 🟡 `bin/lib/run.mjs:1526` — No integration test for `simplifyBlocked → finalize blocked`. The sole gate mechanism has zero test coverage at the integration level.
- 🟡 `bin/lib/run.mjs:1613` — Returns `"done"` when `simplifyBlocked=true` and `blocked=0`. Should return `"blocked"` or `"simplify-blocked"` so callers know finalize was skipped.
- 🟡 `test/simplify-pass.test.mjs:360` — `--no-simplify` test mocks the conditional inline instead of testing the real code path.
- 🟡 `bin/lib/simplify-pass.mjs:176` — Re-verify dispatch returning `ok=false` (vs throwing) has no test. Silent break, distinct from the throw case.
- 🟡 `test/simplify-pass.test.mjs:335` — Role-file-missing test can't exercise the catch branch. Needs `_rolePath` injection.
- 🟡 `bin/lib/simplify-pass.mjs:166` — Diff-empty-after-fixes path untested.

🟡 `bin/lib/simplify-pass.mjs:55` — Diff truncation at 12K chars creates a review blindspot; code beyond the cap is never seen by the reviewer agent. Log full diff size in eval output so operators can assess coverage.

🟡 `bin/lib/simplify-pass.mjs:132` — Fail-open on dispatch error returns `PASS` indistinguishable from a genuine clean review in STATE.json. Consider recording `verdict: "SKIP"` or `bypassed: true` for auditability.

🔴 `simplify-pass.mjs:16` — **Gold-plating: auto-fix loop.** The fix-then-re-verify loop (`MAX_FIX_ROUNDS=2`, lines 82-91 + 144-185) implements ~50 lines of speculative functionality that SPEC.md:96-97 **explicitly declares Out of Scope**: *"Auto-fix loop — the primary cost driver ($298 in review costs). Can be added later as an enhancement."* Also compounds with `buildFixBrief()` having only 1 call site (premature abstraction). **Fix:** Remove the loop; return FAIL immediately on criticals.
🟡 `run.mjs:1519` — `--no-simplify` flag declared Out of Scope per SPEC.md:98
🟡 `simplify-pass.mjs:82` — `buildFixBrief()` has 1 call site (premature abstraction)
🟡 `run.mjs:1507` — Silent skip when `completed===0` has no progress.md entry (inconsistent with `--no-simplify` path)
🟡 `test/simplify-pass.test.mjs:360` — `--no-simplify` test duplicates logic inline, doesn't exercise run.mjs wiring
